title: Symphyotrichum greatae
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum greatae belongs to the family Asteraceae and is classified as a flowering plant. It is a part of the genus Symphyotrichum, which consists of several species of coneflowers. Symphyotrichum greatae is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is often found in dry and sandy areas, and it is known for its large, showy flowers. In terms of its taxonomic classification, Symphyotrichum greatae is a species of flowering plant within the order Asterales.
uses: Symphyotrichum greatae, also known as Great Aster, is not commonly documented for its medicinal uses.
